reasoning: For this question to resolve "yes", 35 or more countries must commit as a matter of treaty obligation to a highly stringent set of five criteria by the end of 2025, including barring patent rights and barring government research funding. As of June 2025, while there is an academic and civil society initiative for an International Non-Use Agreement backed by several nations (e.g., Vanuatu, Mexico, Colombia), there is no drafted, state-level international treaty containing these specific and highly restrictive provisions. International treaties typically take years to negotiate, open for signature, and ratify. Given that no such formal treaty currently exists, it is functionally impossible for one to be drafted, negotiated, and fully ratified by 35 sovereign states within the remaining six months of 2025. Reaching 35 ratifications for any treaty in such a short timeframe without an existing finalized text represents an insurmountable procedural barrier. Therefore, the probability is at the minimum possible bound (0.02).
